             msg["Message-ID"] = make_msgid(domain=DNS_NAME)
         for name, value in self.extra_headers.items():
+            header = name.lower()
+            if header == "bcc":
+                raise ValueError(
+                    'Bcc is not a valid email header. Use the "bcc" '
+                    "argument to specify blind carbon copy recipients."
+                )
             # Avoid headers handled above.
-            if name.lower() not in {"from", "to", "cc", "reply-to"}:
+            if header not in {"from", "to", "cc", "reply-to"}:
                 msg[name] = force_str(value, strings_only=True)
         self._idna_encode_address_header_domains(msg)
         return msg

labeled_issues.description: A Bcc header passed via headers/extra_headers was silently ignored; reviewer required raising ValueError instead, since silently dropping caller-supplied Bcc recipients is incorrect and risky.

reviewer_comments.author: medmunds
reviewer_comments.body:
Django should not silently ignore headers that the caller has added.

```suggestion
            if header == "bcc":
                raise ValueError(
                    "Bcc is not a valid email header. Use the 'bcc' "
                    "argument to specify blind carbon copy recipients."
                )
```

reviewer_comments.author: medmunds
reviewer_comments.body:
I would maybe mention both `extra_headers` and `headers` here. (Confusingly, the `EmailMessage` constructor takes a `headers` *argument* but turns it into an `extra_headers` *attribute* that can be modified at any time before sending.) And maybe clarify that the `bcc` argument is for `EmailMessage`, not the `message()` method.

Maybe something like (this will need to be re-wrapped):

```suggestion
* :meth:`.EmailMessage.message` now raises a ``ValueError`` if ``Bcc`` is
  included in the ``headers`` argument or ``extra_headers`` attribute. Previously, a ``Bcc`` header was
  included in the generated message. Use the :class:`.EmailMessage`\ 's ``bcc`` argument instead.
```

reviewer_comments.author: medmunds
reviewer_comments.body:
```suggestion
    def test_bcc_in_headers_raises_error(self):
```

The docstring not needed. (In new code, Django avoids docstrings for test cases unless they add significant information beyond a descriptive test case name.)

reviewer_comments.author: medmunds
reviewer_comments.body: [note to other reviewers] I *don't* think there would be any value in testing `extra_headers` separately from this test for `headers`. The assertion is scoped to the `message()` method, which covers both.
